text.p: Hello <>

I read your last mail the third time running, today... you know I find it more cumbersome to understand messages in the language in which you wrote that mail. I know I've just given you something to talk about, so fire on.

Perhaps I've lost my sense of speech cos of the on going strike. Nothing to talk about, I suppose. So how many millions were you paid back home. I finally got reimbursed by . So, hope your projeccts are going on smoothly. Strike is still on. Talk some more later, if I do not lose my sense of speech completely before I read from you again. All the best always.
Regards,
Dear Mr. ,
You have my permission to make use of exerpts from my use of Engllish as corpus for your work on Nigerian English.
Sincerely,
Hello Sir,
I hope you're fine and catching up with preparations for your exams. I hope the cold is not unbearable.
I got invited for that summer programme in Lodon ,but got no scholarship. I can't fund it.

By the way,preparations are being made to pay the long awaited monetisation arears. Since it's by e-payment,I suppose your account will be credited.I'm writing from . Nothing to scream about yet .I'll keep you posted .Cheers.
Sincerely,
Hello Sir,
I only just succeeded in using the link you sent me about the 3L Summer school billed for London. I've also sent a reference form to your box for filling and sending.
Tommorow, August 15th is deadline. Pls, oblige me. I'll reach you. Have you applied. Thanks a lot for the links.
Regards,
Hello Sir,
Compliments of the season to you .How are you keeping back in ? Will your family have the priviledge of your presesnce this yuletide? Hope all is well with you.

I badan is yet to come to terms with the fact that I've come of age. As for me I won't get tired of waiting for them, cos I know it's a reality they'll have to face, sooner than they think. So I'm waiting.
Pity, I couldn't make CLAN this time. I look forward to the next meet. and are just as usual. All are hoping to survive. Wish you the best alaways. Cheers.
Regards,
Hello Sir,
Goodmorning Nigerian time. Sorry it's been so busy, I couldn't write a reply to your mail before now. Even this is not much of a reply as I won't be able to respond to all the issues raised.

Monetisation is on hold. No payments have been made yet...trust FGN. First semester exams are on, ASSU is threatening a major strike as from the 22nd June, - nothing to do with monetisation - so the goal is to conclude exams before then.
I suppose your exams are well under way, so I won't trouble your noble head. I'll be in touch. All the best and CHEERS.
Sincerely'
Dear Sir,

I thank you for your mails, and for ever again keeping me abreast of happenings in Linguistics. Concerning the workshop on phonology you mentioned, I doubt if I want to venture out again untill I see the U.I. project to a logical conclusion. ASSU is right now rounding off a warning strike, during which time my daughter was delivered of a baby girl - her first child, my first grandchild, and my mother's first great grandchild. See, one's not getting any younger.

I also got an invitation from Prof. < > to help source young Ph.D students willing to spend one year in Germany. I'll give < > that information. So I just dey. I'm writing you from The Lagos State University Teaching Hospital, where my daughter had her baby. I'm due back in early next week, when the strike will end and preparations for exams commence. Meanwhile, did I tell you I'm the new exams officer - your prayers came to pass, didn't they. I told you a lot was happening, and that's some of it. We're struggling to clear up a lot of mess.
So long Sir. I'll keep in touch. Meanwhile, listen to the advice of the latest grandma on the Black continent, and remain steadfast. I'll be in touch.
Sincerely,
Dear ,

I read your reply to my last mail. The fact about your salary was news to me. I'm sorry. I should however think that you'll benefit from the package I talked about as it's a back log thing. I'll make enquiries along the lines you have stated and feed you back.

Meanwhile, remain as strong, steadfast and undaunted as I know you to be. I'm certain you'll pull through. Things are happening back here in Nigeria, and the department. I'll let you on as they unfold. I'm watching Ibadan closely. Dr. < > sends warm greetings. still has not and did not reimburse me for all kinds of administrative excuses. I'll keep in touch.Cheers.
Sincerely,
Dear ,

How are you? I hope am allowed to say, " long time no hear ." I really hope you're fine, body and soul. I found some time to take a breath and thought I could get in touch with you. How's treating you, and how're you treating . I really wish to read from you.

There's an issue I've avoided raising with you since our return from , which I now feel urged to mention. Soon after return from , I received a mail to the effect that I had reimbursement to collect. I was requested to send originals of my receipts and account number and other information. All this took about two to three months. The mails were singned by one < >. The receipt of my documents was acknowledged, and I was assurred that I would receive re-embursed in the shortest time possible.

Till date, I've not received any further communication on the matter. I didn't think it proper to send a reminder. But I wondered whether you were communicated as well, and thought I could ask you. I would like to know your thoughts on the matter, and if there's anything I should know, I trust you'ld inform me.

I believe you get word regularly from your family. I wish you strength as you rough it out over there, and look forward to seeing you again. <> and I truly miss your presence. Please, be strong........for everyone and for the good. Bye.
Sicerely yours.
